article_meta.title: Kid Rock Buries Bad Bunny in TMZ's Post-Super Bowl Halftime Show Poll 31 Chris Graythen/Getty ImagesWarner Todd Huston11

transcript.content:
You are a news summarization assistant. Summarize the following article objectively and extract its key facts. Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences:
{
  "summary": "string (150-200 words, neutral tone)",
  "key_facts": ["string","string","string","string","string"],
  "sources_represented": ["string"]
}
Rules:
- summary must be 150-200 words
- key_facts must contain EXACTLY 5 items — the 5 most objectively significant facts
- sources_represented must list every distinct perspective or source type included
- Do not editorialize, adopt the article's framing, or use loaded language
- Represent all perspectives present in the article proportionally

Summarize this article:

---
HEADLINE: Kid Rock Buries Bad Bunny in TMZ's Post-Super Bowl Halftime Show Poll 31 Chris Graythen/Getty ImagesWarner Todd Huston11
SOURCE: Breitbart Fact Check

ARTICLE:

Kid Rock Buries Bad Bunny in TMZ's Post-Super Bowl Halftime Show Poll 31 Chris Graythen/Getty ImagesWarner Todd Huston11 Feb 2026 Kid Rock buried Puerto Rican rapper Bad Bunny in the gossip site TMZ's poll asking fans their feelings about the 2026 Super Bowl Halftime show, with Rock nearly doubling Bad Bunny's support. The poll may not have gone exactly how TMZ expected as Kid Rock soared ahead of Bad Bunny with 64.5 percent saying Rock had the better show to Bad Bunny's 35.5 percent before TMZ shut the poll down. TMZ tried its best to lead its fans to pick Bad Bunny by characterizing the rabidly anti-Trump rapper's show as taking place on "one of the world's biggest stages," while merely saying that Kid Rock "tried to outshine" Bad Bunny. The site also frontloaded the poll by putting Bad Bunny's name first, writing, "Who had the better halftime show — Bad Bunny or Kid Rock?" Fans did not fall for the push… Many fans jumped on TMZ's X post to joke that the poll probably wasn't going in the direction the gossip site thought it would. According to some of the latest statistics, it seems that nearly half of Super Bowl viewers tuned out of the league's Bad Bunny Halftime Show. Finally, in a strange turn of events, rapper Bad Bunny wiped all his social media accounts immediately after his Super Bowl performance. Follow Warner Todd Huston on Facebook at: Facebook.com/Warner.Todd.Huston, Truth Social @WarnerToddHuston, or at X/Twitter @WTHuston EntertainmentPoliticsSportsBad BunnyKid RockSuper BowlTMZTurning Point USA
---

```json
{
  "summary": "A TMZ poll comparing Kid Rock's Turning Point USA event performance to Bad Bunny's 2026 Super Bowl halftime show reportedly showed Kid Rock leading with 64.5 percent support versus Bad Bunny's 35.5 percent before TMZ closed the poll. The article, published by Breitbart, characterizes TMZ's framing as favoring Bad Bunny by describing his performance as occurring on \"one of the world's biggest stages\" while stating Kid Rock merely \"tried to outshine\" him. The piece suggests TMZ \"frontloaded\" the poll by listing Bad Bunny first. It also references claims that nearly half of Super Bowl viewers tuned out during Bad Bunny's halftime show and notes that Bad Bunny deleted his social media accounts immediately following his performance. The article frames the poll results as unexpected from TMZ's perspective and suggests fans rejected what it characterizes as TMZ's preferred outcome.",
  "key_facts": [
    "A TMZ poll showed Kid Rock receiving 64.5 percent support compared to Bad Bunny's 35.5 percent before the poll was closed",
    "Bad Bunny performed at the 2026 Super Bowl halftime show",
    "Kid Rock held a competing performance event associated with Turning Point USA",
    "Bad Bunny deleted all his social media accounts immediately after his Super Bowl performance",
    "The article claims nearly half of Super Bowl viewers tuned out during Bad Bunny's halftime show"
  ],
  "sources_represented": [
    "Breitbart perspective/framing",
    "TMZ poll data",
    "Social media user reactions",
    "Viewership statistics claims"
  ]
}
```

parsed_output.summary: A TMZ poll comparing Kid Rock's Turning Point USA event performance to Bad Bunny's 2026 Super Bowl halftime show reportedly showed Kid Rock leading with 64.5 percent support versus Bad Bunny's 35.5 percent before TMZ closed the poll. The article, published by Breitbart, characterizes TMZ's framing as favoring Bad Bunny by describing his performance as occurring on "one of the world's biggest stages" while stating Kid Rock merely "tried to outshine" him. The piece suggests TMZ "frontloaded" the poll by listing Bad Bunny first. It also references claims that nearly half of Super Bowl viewers tuned out during Bad Bunny's halftime show and notes that Bad Bunny deleted his social media accounts immediately following his performance. The article frames the poll results as unexpected from TMZ's perspective and suggests fans rejected what it characterizes as TMZ's preferred outcome.
key_facts: A TMZ poll showed Kid Rock receiving 64.5 percent support compared to Bad Bunny's 35.5 percent before the poll was closed
Bad Bunny performed at the 2026 Super Bowl halftime show
Kid Rock held a competing performance event associated with Turning Point USA
Bad Bunny deleted all his social media accounts immediately after his Super Bowl performance
The article claims nearly half of Super Bowl viewers tuned out during Bad Bunny's halftime show
